adjusted.reasoning: Closest QF and the model's shakiest number: model has England 63% but the 90-minute market is a near coin-flip (ENG ~50/draw ~26/NOR ~24), so anchored to market. England have the deeper squad, calmer defence and Bellingham in form after his Mexico masterclass; Norway are Haaland-dependent (7 goals in 4) with big variance. England lose the suspended Quansah (red vs Mexico) and Reece James is a fitness doubt, so Konsa likely at RB — a spot Norway will target, which is why we sit at market ~50% rather than higher. Norway's Ryerson also a fitness doubt. Predicted 1-2: open, high-scoring, England edge with Haaland scoring.

factors.factor: bet365 ENG even-money / NOR +250 / draw +260; FanDuel ENG -115 (de-vig 90min ~ENG 49/draw 26/NOR 25)
Quansah suspended (red vs Mexico); Reece James fitness doubt -> Konsa likely RB, Norway target; Bellingham in form
Haaland 7 goals in 4; Ryerson (Norway RB) a fitness doubt after missing games

odds.source: consensus bet365/FanDuel QF (home NOR +285, X +260, away ENG -108)

posted_to.note: QF pre-bet sweep (all 4 biddable), R16-sweep precedent. PROTECT mode (rank 1/34, lead +12, rem_grp=0), k=0.3 -> contest pick == EP-max on adjusted (market-anchored). Matchday-morning research may re-bet via superseded_by. Locked 2026-07-08 pre-kickoff.

adjusted.reasoning: Argentina favourites but the model's 85% is far above the market (~55%), so anchored to de-vigged consensus (ARG 55/27/18). Argentina looked fragile in R16 — 2-0 down to Egypt before a late comeback, and Messi has now missed two penalties this tournament (a WC first) — so no push above market. Still, Messi leads the Golden Boot on 8 and the attack is elite. Switzerland are a first QF since 1954 on the back of a low-event 0-0 + shootout over Colombia: defensively resilient but tired legs from 120 minutes and short on proven attacking threat. Predicted 2-0.

factors.factor: DK ARG -140 / draw +250 / SUI +425; BetMGM ARG -145 (de-vig ~ARG 55/27/18)
Argentina fragile in R16 (2-0 down to Egypt, comeback); Messi missed 2 pens (WC first) but leads Golden Boot on 8
Switzerland first QF since 1954, resilient but tired from 120min+pens vs Colombia, limited attack
